Round 6 · This Saturday YPFL 2026

HOME OF THE BULLDOGS.

159 winters on the Yorke Peninsula. Founded at the Globe Inn, June 17, 1867. Never merged, never folded. This is country footy at its purest.

Visiting Wallaroo Oval

Game day,made simple.

Whether it's your first time at Wallaroo Oval or your fiftieth, here's what you need to know about a Bulldogs home Saturday on Cornish Terrace.

Free entry · Family-friendly · All welcome

⏱

Gates Open

Junior matches kick off from 9am. A-Grade bounce 2:40pm.

Saturdays, April–August
🅿

Parking & Entry

Free parking within the grounds. Gate entrance fee applies on match day.

Easy access · Plenty of space
🍔

Canteen

Chicken burgers, hot chips, nuggets, pies, pasties, sausage rolls, hot dogs, fresh rolls and sushi. Drinks and lollies too.

Run by club volunteers
♿

Accessibility

Ground-level access throughout. Accessible toilets in the clubrooms. Reserved viewing area on the wing.

Contact us for assistance
🐕

Dogs Welcome

Well-behaved dogs on lead are welcome on the boundary. Water bowls at the canteen.

Dogs of Bulldogs supporters
🎯

After the Siren

Clubrooms open post-match. Meals, licensed bar, club activities and presentations.

Members draw on the bell

Social events are hosted at the venue listed. Some rounds align with hotels & sponsor partners; others are held at opposition club venues for after-match catch-ups.

01

Play

Seniors. Juniors. Auskick. The doors are open. 2026 player registration is live via PlayHQ — new players welcome at every grade.

Register now
02

Sponsor

Five tiers. 70+ active partners. Be seen on jumpers, boundary signage, programmes and digital channels every week of the season.

Sponsor prospectus
03

Belong

Five tiers from $50 Family to $500 Patron. Plus our $70 Long Distance tier — for Bulldogs faithful living more than 100km from Wallaroo Oval.

Become a member
The Pathway

Five & up. From Auskickto A-Grade.

A clear path from your child's first kick on the Wallaroo Oval grass to running out for the Bulldogs A-Grade. Every stage. Every Saturday. Every winter.

Auskick
Ages 5–7
First Kicks
Saturday morning fundamentals. No scores, no pressure. Just running, kicking and laughing.
U9
Ages 7–9
Under 9
First match-day jumper. Bridging Auskick and competition footy with modified rules and a 9am bounce.
U11
Ages 9–11
Under 11
Modified rules and full grades. First taste of YPFL competition with weekly fixtures.
U14
Ages 12–14
Under 14
Full-field football, structured training. Where many lifelong Bulldogs careers begin.
U17
Ages 15–17
Under 17
Senior pathway prep. Pre-season with the B Grade. Some debut for A-Grade in their final year.
A-Grade
18+
Senior Football
B Grade and A-Grade. The same red, white and blue your dad wore. And his dad before him.
The Heart of the Club

Country footy runs onvolunteer hands.

Every Saturday at Wallaroo Oval, dozens of volunteers make it happen. The canteen, the gate, the boundary, the scoreboard, the laundry, the recovery breakfasts. None of it happens without the people who put their hands up.

If you've got an hour, an afternoon, or a season to spare — we'd love to have you. No experience needed. We'll teach you everything.

Where we need help in 2026

  • Saturday canteen 2–3 hour shifts
  • Goal umpires & boundary Per match
  • Junior team manager Season role
  • Match-day photography As available
  • Bulldog Ball committee Pre-event
  • Working bees & ground prep Pre-season & ad-hoc
I'd like to help→
1867
The Heritage

Founded at the Globe Inn,17 June 1867.

About a dozen members joined and paid their subs — then had a few preliminary kicks in the park with a first-rate ball obtained from Adelaide.

159 years later, we're still here. Never merged, never folded. The Seagulls became the Bulldogs in the late 1960s, and red joined the blue and white. Everything else, as they say, is history.

1867
Year founded.
One of the oldest in Australia.
22
A-Grade premierships
1889 to 2000.
5
Senior Colts flags
since 2014.
∞
Stories told
at the Bulldogs Club.
The Trophy Cabinet

22 flags. 138 years.

From 1889 to 2000, the Bulldogs lifted the A-Grade premiership cup twenty-two times. Every year red, every year a flag, every year a story still told at the clubrooms.

Wallaroo FC
The Bulldogs · Est. 1867
One of Australia's oldest continuously running football clubs. Playing in red, white and blue on the Yorke Peninsula since the late 1960s — and on the same patch of ground since 1867.

Acknowledgment of Country
Wallaroo Football Club acknowledges the Narungga people as the Traditional Custodians of the lands on which we play, train and gather. We pay our respects to Elders past and present, and recognise the continuing connection of First Nations people to the country, waters and culture of the Yorke Peninsula.
